front: What are the three main categories of learning resources for Green IT?
back: Primary categories include: (1) Printed/Electronic Books & Textbooks, (2) Journals & Research Papers, (3) Online Digital Resources (courses, webinars, podcasts)

front: Why are peer-reviewed journals important for Green IT studies?
back: Peer-reviewed journals provide current, validated research findings, ensure academic credibility, and help students stay updated with latest sustainability practices in IT.

front: Name two reputed journals for Green IT research
back: (1) Journal of Sustainable Computing, (2) IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Computing

front: What is the role of MOOCs in Green IT education?
back: MOOCs provide flexible, accessible online courses from top universities, offering self-paced learning, certifications, and interactive content on sustainability topics.

front: Why are case studies important in Green IT learning?
back: Case studies provide real-world examples of sustainable IT implementations, helping students understand practical applications, challenges, and solutions in actual organizations.

front: What is the significance of government policy documents in Green IT studies?
back: Government documents provide regulatory frameworks, sustainability standards, national policies on e-waste, energy efficiency mandates, and environmental compliance guidelines.

front: What are ISO/IEC standards relevant to Green IT?
back: ISO 14001 (Environmental Management), ISO 50001 (Energy Management), and IEC 62430 (Environmentally Conscious Design) are key standards for sustainable IT practices.

front: How do professional organizations contribute to Green IT learning?
back: Professional bodies like ACM, IEEE, and green computing groups provide networking, certifications, conferences, white papers, and industry best practices.

front: What is the purpose of simulation labs in Green IT education?
back: Simulation labs allow students to practice energy-efficient algorithm design, virtual machine optimization, and carbon footprint calculations in safe experimental environments.

front: What are the benefits of webinar-based learning for Green IT?
back: Webinars offer expert insights, live Q&A sessions, case study presentations, and interaction with industry professionals without geographical constraints.

front: Why are textbooks important for foundational Green IT knowledge?
back: Textbooks provide structured curriculum, systematic explanations, chapter exercises, and comprehensive coverage of core concepts aligned with academic syllabi.

front: What role do white papers play in Green IT education?
back: White papers offer in-depth technical analysis, vendor-neutral solutions, industry trends, and detailed explorations of specific sustainability technologies.

front: What are the advantages of collaborative learning platforms?
back: Collaborative platforms enable peer discussions, group projects, knowledge sharing, and collective problem-solving on sustainability challenges.

front: What is the importance of conference proceedings in Green IT research?
back: Conference proceedings present cutting-edge research, new methodologies, networking opportunities, and early-stage findings before formal journal publication.

front: How do podcasts contribute to Green IT awareness?
back: Podcasts provide convenient audio learning, expert interviews, industry updates, and sustainability discussions during commutes or multitasking.
